title: The Flame in the Wind
content:
Dost thou burn low and tremble, all but die? 
And dost thou fear in darkness to be whirled? 
Nay, flame, thou art mine immortality, 
The wind is but the passing of the world!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Margaret Steele Anderson, titled "The Flame in the Wind", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
